editor_settings.title: Did Target Really Sell a Canon G7X Mark II for $80? Truth or Lie
editor_settings.content:
Did Target Really Sell a Canon G7X Mark II for $80? Truth or Lie

A TikTok is claiming someone got a Canon G7 X Mark II from Target for $80.

That sounds insane because, honestly, it probably is.

Here is the clean read before you waste a whole afternoon refreshing Target like it owes you money.

The TikTok That Started It

@lily1278kh5 Canon g7x goals 😭 tutorial is pinned 💙 #target #studentdiscount #targetfinds #canong7x #canon ♬ Beauty And A Beat - Justin Bieber

Verdict: Mostly Lie, Or Missing A Lot Of Context

I would not treat this as a normal Target deal.

The Canon G7 X Mark II is not a cheap little toy camera. Canon's own US product page has listed the G7 X Mark II at $799.99 and out of stock. Target search results are showing the newer G7 X Mark III around $879.99, not a real $80 Mark II listing.

That makes the $80 claim very suspicious.

Could somebody have paid $80 out of pocket after gift cards, rewards, employee discounts, a return adjustment, or a weird one-off store issue? Sure. Retail receipts can be messy.

But that is different from saying Target is selling the Canon G7 X Mark II for $80.

Why The $80 Number Does Not Pass The Smell Test

The used market alone makes this hard to believe.

The G7 X Mark II is still hyped because it gives creators the look they want: compact body, flip screen, 1-inch sensor, built-in flash, and that smoother point-and-shoot photo style people keep chasing on TikTok.

That demand keeps prices high. A clean working unit usually does not fall anywhere near $80 unless something weird is going on.

  • It may have been a different cheap digital camera
  • It may have involved coupons, gift cards, or store credit
  • It may have been an open-box or return edge case
  • It may have been a pricing error that regular buyers cannot repeat
  • It may simply be missing the full receipt context

That last one is the big point. Viral deal videos often show the fun part, not the boring explanation.

What Target Actually Shows

Target does sell Canon compact cameras, but the visible G7 X listing people keep running into is usually the G7 X Mark III, not the Mark II.

And the Mark III is not remotely $80. It has been listed around $879.99 at Target, with some third-party or special listings going higher.

So if someone says, "just go to Target and get the G7X Mark II for $80," no. Do not plan your day around that.

The Realistic Price To Watch

If you actually want the Canon G7 X Mark II, a believable deal is still in the few-hundred-dollar range.

Our current G7 X Mark II pick is priced at $320, which is far more realistic than the TikTok number and still much lower than the inflated prices people often chase online.

[product-card:canon-powershot-g7-x-mark-ii-201mp-digital-camera-7x-zoom-95-new]


Should You Chase The $80 Target Trick?

No, not as your main plan.

If you already shop at Target and happen to see a clearance tag, scan it. Fine. But do not assume there is a repeatable student discount trick that turns a hyped Canon camera into an $80 checkout.

That is how people lose hours hunting a deal that probably was never public in the first place.

Final Answer

The TikTok might show a real moment, but the claim is not reliable as a buying strategy.

Truth or lie? Mostly lie for regular shoppers.

If the goal is actually getting a Canon G7 X Mark II, ignore the $80 fantasy and compare real working units instead. Anything clean, tested, and around the low $300s is already a much more believable win.
